experiences.intentions: Key state: 4x4 board is nearly full with only one empty space at bottom-right. Highest tile 128 sits at row2,col2, and the board is arranged so larger values are concentrated toward the upper-left. Main threat is making a move that shuffles this structure or creates no useful merges. Candidate actions: Up appears invalid or at least produces no merges because every column is blocked by differing tiles. Left also seems to make no movement on the top three rows and only shifts the bottom row slightly without creating merges, so it likely worsens flexibility. Down would move tiles away from the current strong upper-left concentration and break the favorable monotonic structure. Right keeps the top rows fixed while using the empty bottom-right space to slide the bottom row right, preserving the high-value cluster and maintaining future options. Goal now is survival while preserving the 128 anchor and board order; sub-goal is choose the least disruptive legal move. Therefore right is best.

experiences.intentions: No empty spaces, so only moves that create merges are legal/useful. Checking rows/columns: left/right create no adjacent equal merges in any row. Up has a merge in column 1 at the bottom (2+2), while down also merges that same column. Since the highest tile 128 is near the upper area, moving up preserves stronger tiles toward the top and opens space without pulling the 128 downward. Goal is to survive and keep board structure stable while creating an empty cell; up best supports that.
